title: Serias

{{Quote|With first contact with any lifeforms on Serias still a question and only an incomplete survey of the system itself, I worry about remnants of the Empire or other unsavory types hearing about the unusual relics or mineral samples that have been discovered and attempting to exploit the Serianan Belt.|Korren Starchaser's datafiles|<ref name="SD" />}}
'''Serias''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Serianan system/Legends|Serianan system]] in the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The lone inhabited world in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]], Serias was theorized by the [[Bureau of Scouting and Exploration Services|New Republic Scout Service]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Korren Starchaser]] to be the home of a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Theoretical Serias species|species]]. Upon [[Serias expedition|exploring]] the Serianan system, Starchaser's [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] team only gave the planet a cursory inspection without making any contact with the local civilization due to the already amassed information from the nearby [[Serianan Belt]] of [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]].

==Description==
{{Quote|The only planet in the system, Serias, maintains conditions capable of supporting standard life forms […]|Korren Starchaser's datafiles|<ref name="SD" />}}
Serias was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was located within the remote [[Serianan system/Legends|Serianan system]],<ref name="SD" /> a part of the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The only planet in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]], Serias was sufficiently large and located close enough to its [[Star/Legends|star]] to be habitable by standard life forms. The planet gave its name to the [[Serianan Belt]], an expansive [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] that took up more than half of the star system, and a [[Unidentified pirate base|pirate base]] was also located nearby.<ref name="SD" />

==History==
{{Quote|With the wealth of data from the Gulch and the desire to decipher this information before going on to make first contact, our team opted to perform only a brief inspection of the planet Serias, and hard data on the terrestrial civilization is unavailable at this point.|Korren Starchaser's datafiles|<ref name="SD" />}}
At some point during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] reign, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bureau of Scouting and Exploration Services|New Republic Scout Service]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Korren Starchaser]] and his team [[Serias expedition|explored]] the Serianan system in the [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Jedi Dreamer]]''. However, they decided to only make a short inspection of Serias, without even scanning it with [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], because of the abundance of information the team had already collected from several [[Serianan runes|markings]] and [[Serianan statue|statues]] on some of the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] in the Serianan Belt as well as the desire to inspect that data before making any contact with the civilization that existed on Serias. Starchaser later included a report on the exploration of the Serianan system in his [[Datafile/Legends|datafiles]].<ref name="SD" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|A few of the smaller marks also seem to indicate humanoid beings, although judging from the preliminary scans, the inhabitants of Serias are most likely quite biologically different from Humans.|Korren Starchaser's datafiles|<ref name="SD" />}}
Serias was an inhabited world, and the discovery of advanced markings and statues on the asteroids led Captain Starchaser to theorize that a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]—albeit probably [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]]—[[Theoretical Serias species|species]] lived on the planet.<ref name="SD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Serias was introduced in the "[[Scouts' Dispatch]]" feature of [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]], published in [[November]] [[1995]]. The feature was written by [[Peter Woodworth]].<ref name="SD" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the planet in grid square P-2.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
